title: behavioral/typeclass search
manager: Search the registry by behavior, not just by name — find a machine by the interface it satisfies or the properties it proves, a typeclass-style query over what a machine does rather than what it happens to be called. This is what makes a registry of verified machines actually useful: you ask for "something that behaves like a rate limiter with these guarantees" and get proof-carrying matches, not a keyword lottery you then have to audit by hand.
cs: behavioral/typeclass search over the registry; query by satisfied interface / proven property; trust tiers target-qualified (never bare); competing adjuncts; policy-constrained resolution (SAT/CSP); behavioral semver via proof-dependency invalidation.
